body: AI has often carried a stigma — people think it can't look good, and that it's limited to slow zooms and wide sci-fi landscapes. The challenge I gave myself was to break that perception by creating camera movements that felt natural and cinematic, all while telling a story that felt grounded and real.
Most AI content online tends to be short clips without much depth or meaning. I set out to go further — building a real story and fully realized world using nothing but text-to-video prompting.

feature.caption: Lost and Found — AI short film.

body: I challenged myself to create a 100% AI-generated short film in just six days — to learn the tools firsthand, explore the limits of what AI was capable of, and see how far I could push the technology creatively.

I wrote and storyboarded a post-apocalyptic thriller following a dog, a robot, and a little girl on a haunting journey. Using a range of tools — primarily Google's Veo 2 — I generated every single clip, with a huge part of the process being R&D on prompt engineering to achieve the right visual fidelity and consistency across environments and characters.
led: Wrote, storyboarded, and directed the film

impact.note: After posting on LinkedIn, the film quickly gained traction — over a million impressions, 100+ shares and reposts, and several new job opportunities within the week. The lessons learned continue to shape how I approach AI-driven storytelling.
